measurement_method: Navigation Timing API for navigate latency; JS timestamp deltas (minus ~6150ms baseline for 2 JS round-trips) for tool call durations; payload_bytes estimated from response character count

scenarios.name: Simple Navigation (example.com)
notes: chrome: operates on existing Chrome instance (warm cache); Playwright paths use fresh headless Chromium (cold start).
read_page returns accessibility tree; get_page_text returns raw text extraction.

scenarios.name: Google Search
notes: chrome: operates on existing Chrome instance (warm cache); Playwright paths use fresh headless Chromium (cold start).
Google search results have extremely complex DOM; read_page at depth 2 still produces ~12KB accessibility tree.
form_input set value but did not trigger search submission; navigate used directly for search results URL.

scenarios.name: YouTube Search
notes: chrome: operates on existing Chrome instance (warm cache); Playwright paths use fresh headless Chromium (cold start).
YouTube homepage includes video player elements and recommendations; search results page loaded via direct URL navigation.
YouTube search results navigate was fast (1246ms) likely due to SPA-style client-side routing from warm YouTube session.
